plant.description: Unique History: One of the oldest spices/herbs documented in human history. Mentioned in the Vedas and ancient Ayurvedic texts for its unmatched 'Dipana-Pachana' (digestive) and 'Trishnahara' (thirst-quencher) providing properties. It is culturally used in all major cuisines and for producing the world's most common digestive spice. Physical Characteristics: A small, annual herb growing up to 30-60 cm in height, with thin, hollow stems and attractive, twice-pinnate leaves. It features small, white or pale-pink flowers arranged in terminal umbels. The fruit is a globose, yellowish-brown schizocarp (seed) containing two seeds. It is found throughout India and tropical/sub-tropical regions worldwide. Specific Chemical Compounds: Primarily contains Essential Oil (Linalool, Pinene, Cymene), Fatty Acids (Petroselinic acid), and Flavonoids (Quercetin). The presence of linalool is crucial for its cooling, anti-septic, and neuro-relaxant action. Strict Standardizations: The seed is standardized in API based on macroscopic features (fragrant, yellowish-brown globes). Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<6%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>10%), and water-soluble extractive (>15%). HPTLC quantification is used to monitor linalool content.

rasa_taste: Kashaya (astringent), Tikta (bitter) - Green; Madhura (sweet), Katu (pungent) - Seed.
ayurvedic_properties.dosha_karma: Powerful Tridoshahara (pacifies all three doshas), specifically excels in balancing Pitta and Vata. Due to its Madhura (sweet) and Tikta (bitter) Rasa and Sheeta (cooling) Virya, it is the most prestigious herb for 'Dipana' (appetizer) and 'Trishnahara' (thirst-relieving) therapies, clearing the fiery and agitated qualities of Pitta. It does not aggravate Vata when used appropriately.

cold_infusion__hima_.method: 10g of crushed seeds soaked in 60ml water overnight; filtered and taken with sugar in the morning for cooling and acidity. This is the gold standard for Pitta issues.

seed_powder__churna_.method: 3-6g of fine powder taken twice daily with warm water or buttermilk (for general digestion).

seed_decoction__kwatha_.method: 20g of seeds boiled in 160ml water reduced to 40ml for chronic urinary issues and fevers. Take twice daily before meals.

fresh_leaf_juice__swarasa_.method: 10-20ml of fresh juice taken twice daily for managing blood pressure and cooling the body.

external_paste.method: Ground fresh leaves with water into a fine paste; applied directly to the forehead for headache and to skin for acne to reduce heat.

dosage.children: Generally safe. 1-2 ml of Hima or 125-250 mg of powder mixed with honey twice daily for cooling during infections.
dosage.adults: Powder: 3-6 grams; Decoction: 20-50 ml; Seed Hima: 50 ml, divided throughout the day before meals.
dosage.elderly: Ideal for age-related digestive support and maintaining cardiac health. Dosage: 3g of powder daily with warm water at bedtime.

medicinal_uses: Expert Digestive and Cooling Agent: Dhanyaka is the premier Ayurvedic remedy for all conditions of acidity and internal heat (Pitta). Its Sheeta (cooling) nature helps in relieving 'Daha' (burning sensation) and providing instant relief from acid reflux, gastritis, and digestive cramps. WHO-aligned research supports its antispasmodic and carminative effects.

Urinary and Kidney Care: Acts as a powerful agent for managing urinary tract infections (UTI). It helps in reducing burning sensation during micturition and promoting the excretion of toxins by cooling the Pitta in the bladder and kidneys. It is traditionally used in 'Mutra-kricchra' management.

Management of Thirst and Dehydration: Known as 'Trishnahara' (thirst-reliever). It is used to quench excessive thirst, particularly in fevers and during summer, through its cooling and metabolic balancing action.

Heart and Cardiovascular Health: Acts as a mild Hridya (heart-tonic). It helps in regulating heart rate and protecting the heart muscles from oxidative stress through its potent antioxidant and cholesterol-lowering profile (Hridya-rogahara).

Hormonal and Menstrual Support: Traditionally used to manage excessive menstrual flow (Asrigdara) and internal bleeding (Raktapitta). Its cooling and astringent properties help in providing systemic hormonal balance.

Skin Health and Wound Healing: Its antimicrobial and blood-purifying properties help in treating chronic skin conditions and localized inflammation. The leaf juice applied topically promotes rapid relief from acne and sun-burns.

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Linalool (up to 70%), Geraniol, Pinene, Terpinene, and diversos essential oils that provide its multifaceted medicinal benefits, particularly its potent anti-septic and anti-inflammatory properties.

safety_profile.warning: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe medicinal herb with a history of use spanning millennia across various global cuisines and traditional systems (Ayurveda, Unani, and TCM) without documented toxicity.
safety_profile.medical_supervision: Use under the guidance of a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
contraindications: Generally very safe. However, individuals with high Kapha (cold, excess mucus) should use it in moderation as it is Sheeta (cooling). Avoid during active extreme constipation due to its 'Grahi' (absorbent) nature.
possible_side_effects: None documented at traditional therapeutic levels. Excessive use in very sensitive individuals may rarely cause mild skin cooling or stomach dryness.
drug_interactions: Monitor if taken alongside synthetic anti-diuretic or sedative medications as it may potentiate their effects. No severe adverse interactions reported in WHO monographs.

plant_profile.note: AI estimated profile based on known properties
